title: pygeum vulgare
intro: Pygeum vulgare is a plant species native to Europe and Western Asia, also known as the African cherry or prunus africanum, and is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family. The medicinal properties of Pygeum vulgare are attributed to its bark, which contains a group of compounds called ferulic acid and a variety of sterols, including beta-sitosterol, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pygeum vulgare include supporting prostate health, reducing the risk of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), and alleviating symptoms such as urinary frequency and nocturia. Pygeum vulgare is commonly used in herbal preparations, including capsules, tablets, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs, such as saw palmetto and stinging nettle, to enhance its medicinal effects.
